nameMeanings.name: Rufus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Ruff (see Rough ), with post-medieval excrescent -(e)s. The spelling of the surname has been modified to look like the personal name Rufus, which came into vernacular use in England in the 19th century. This personal name, however, may be the origin of the surname among African Americans (see 3 below).

African (mainly Nigeria) and probably also African American: from the personal name Rufus, borne by various minor early Christian saints. Originally it was a Latin nickname for someone with red hair (see 2 above).
Slovak (mainly Rúfus): nickname for a red-haired man, from Latin rufus ‘red(dish), red-haired’.

variants.name: Ruff
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English rough, rowe, ruwe ‘rough (haired), shaggy, disheveled’.
English (of Norman origin): perhaps a post-medieval spelling of   Roff   or   Roof  ; see   Rolfe  .
Scottish: perhaps a Scots pronunciation of   Rock  .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

en.lifeSummary: Alan Rufus Count of Richmond I was born in 1047, in Saint-Pierre-Quiberon, Morbihan, Brittany, France as the son of Eozen Penteur. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1119, at the age of 72, and was buried in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ja.Bury St Edmunds Abbey: イギリスイギリススフォルクブリー スト エドムンズ
ja.Saint-Pierre-Quiberon, Bretagne, France: フランスブレタグンモービハンサイント-パエレ-クイバーオン
ja.lifeSummary: Alan Rufus Count of Richmond Iは1047年にフランスブレタグンモービハンサイント-パエレ-クイバーオンでEozen Penteurの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1119年に72歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクブリー スト エドムンズにて埋葬された。

ko.Bury St Edmunds Abbey: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Alan Rufus Count of Richmond I(은)는 1047년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 세인트-피레-키베런에서 Eozen Penteur의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1119년 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에 묻혔다.

ru.Bury St Edmunds Abbey: бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Alan Rufus Count of Richmond I родился в 1047 г. в местности сеинт-пьирре-куибирон, морбихань, бретагн, Франция. Его отца звали Eozen Penteur. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1119 г. в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
